Document Title: Third-Party Service Agreement Template - Kahana Oasis
Version: 1.0
Effective Date: March 2026
Owner: Security Lead / Compliance Lead
Approved By: CEO / CTO

IMPORTANT:
- This is an internal template and must be reviewed by legal counsel before execution.
- Use together with the Vendor Risk Assessment Template and vendor security review evidence.
- For vendors processing personal data, attach a DPA and reference it in Section 9.

------------------------------------------------------------
THIRD-PARTY SERVICE AGREEMENT
------------------------------------------------------------

This Third-Party Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into by and between:

Customer: Kahana Labs, Inc. ("Kahana")
and
Vendor: [LEGAL VENDOR NAME] ("Vendor")

Effective Date: [DATE]
Term: [INITIAL TERM] with [RENEWAL TERMS]

1) PURPOSE AND SCOPE
1.1 Vendor will provide the services described in Exhibit A ("Services").
1.2 Services are limited to approved use cases for Kahana Oasis products and operations.
1.3 Vendor must not process Kahana data outside documented and approved scope.

2) DEFINITIONS
2.1 "Kahana Data" means all data provided by or generated for Kahana, including customer data, usage events, support data, and derived metadata.
2.2 "Personal Data" has the meaning assigned by applicable privacy/data protection law.
2.3 "Security Incident" means unauthorized access, disclosure, destruction, alteration, or loss of Kahana Data, or suspected compromise of systems processing Kahana Data.
2.4 "Subprocessor" means any third party engaged by Vendor that processes Kahana Data.

3) SERVICE REQUIREMENTS

3.1 Vendor will provide Services in accordance with documented service descriptions, support commitments, and any agreed service levels in Exhibit B.
3.2 Vendor will maintain operational capacity and resilience suitable to the service criticality.
3.3 Vendor will notify Kahana of material changes to service architecture, hosting region, or subprocessors before such changes take effect where feasible.

4) INFORMATION SECURITY OBLIGATIONS
4.1 Vendor shall maintain a written information security program aligned with recognized standards (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001, or equivalent controls).
4.2 Vendor shall implement administrative, technical, and physical controls including:
  (a) Encryption in transit using TLS 1.2+;
  (b) Encryption at rest for Kahana Data;
  (c) Role-based access control and least privilege;
  (d) MFA for privileged/admin access;
  (e) Logging and monitoring of security-relevant events;
  (f) Vulnerability and patch management processes;
  (g) Backup and recovery controls where Vendor stores critical Kahana Data.
4.3 Vendor shall provide, upon request, current security documentation and independent assurance artifacts (e.g., SOC reports, ISO certificates, penetration summaries, or equivalent evidence).

5) INCIDENT NOTIFICATION AND RESPONSE
5.1 Vendor will notify Kahana without undue delay and no later than [24/48] hours after confirming a Security Incident affecting Kahana Data.
5.2 Notification must include known scope, impacted data categories, containment actions, and remediation plan.
5.3 Vendor will cooperate with Kahana investigation, evidence requests, and customer/regulatory notification obligations as applicable.
5.4 Vendor will provide a written post-incident report within [10] business days (or mutually agreed period).

6) AUDIT, ASSURANCE, AND EVIDENCE RIGHTS
6.1 Vendor will provide current independent assurance reports (SOC 2 Type II preferred for critical vendors, SOC 2 Type I acceptable with compensating review where appropriate).
6.2 If reports are unavailable or materially limited, Kahana may request additional due diligence responses and control evidence.
6.3 Kahana may reassess Vendor risk at least annually for medium/high-risk vendors and upon material changes or incidents.

7) DATA HANDLING, RETENTION, AND DELETION
7.1 Vendor shall process Kahana Data only on documented instructions from Kahana and solely for purposes in this Agreement.
7.2 Vendor will maintain data retention and deletion procedures and support Kahana requests for deletion/export within agreed timelines.
7.3 Upon termination or Kahana request, Vendor shall return and/or securely delete Kahana Data, unless retention is legally required.
7.4 If legal retention applies, Vendor must continue applicable confidentiality and security protections.

8) SUBPROCESSORS
8.1 Vendor will maintain an up-to-date subprocessor list and provide notice of intended changes.
8.2 Vendor remains fully responsible for subprocessor performance and compliance with this Agreement.
8.3 Vendor shall flow down equivalent data protection and security obligations to subprocessors.

9) DATA PROCESSING ADDENDUM (DPA)
9.1 If Vendor processes Personal Data for Kahana, the parties shall execute a DPA that forms part of this Agreement.
9.2 The DPA shall include, as applicable:
  (a) Subject matter and duration of processing;
  (b) Nature and purpose of processing;
  (c) Categories of data subjects and Personal Data;
  (d) Security measures;
  (e) Subprocessor terms;
  (f) Cross-border transfer mechanisms where needed;
  (g) Data subject rights assistance;
  (h) Breach notification and cooperation duties.
9.3 In case of conflict between this Agreement and the DPA on Personal Data processing terms, the DPA controls.

10) COMPLIANCE WITH LAW
10.1 Vendor will comply with applicable data protection, privacy, and information security laws relevant to Services.
10.2 Vendor will promptly notify Kahana of material legal or regulatory actions that could impact service delivery or data protection commitments.

11) CONFIDENTIALITY
11.1 Each party will protect Confidential Information with at least reasonable care, and not less than the care used to protect its own similar information.
11.2 Confidential Information may only be used to perform obligations under this Agreement.

12) BUSINESS CONTINUITY AND DISASTER RECOVERY
12.1 Vendor shall maintain BC/DR capabilities proportionate to criticality of Services.
12.2 Upon request, Vendor will provide a summary of BC/DR testing cadence and recent test outcomes.
12.3 Where vendor service materially affects Kahana operations, Vendor agrees to support restoration objectives defined in Exhibit B or service documentation.

13) TERMINATION AND EXIT ASSISTANCE
13.1 Kahana may terminate for material breach not cured within [30] days.
13.2 Upon termination/expiration, Vendor will provide reasonable transition assistance and data portability/export support for [30-90] days as agreed.

14) LIABILITY AND INDEMNIFICATION
14.1 Liability caps, exclusions, and indemnification terms are set forth in the commercial order form/MSA.
14.2 Nothing limits obligations for confidentiality breaches, data protection violations, or willful misconduct to the extent restricted by law.

15) GOVERNING TERMS
15.1 Order of precedence: (1) DPA for Personal Data terms, (2) this Agreement, (3) exhibits/order form, unless otherwise stated in writing.
15.2 This Agreement may be executed electronically and in counterparts.

------------------------------------------------------------
EXHIBIT A - SERVICE DESCRIPTION (Template)
------------------------------------------------------------
Vendor Service Name:
Service Category:
Use Case at Kahana:
Data Categories In Scope:
Hosting/Processing Region(s):
Production Access Required (Y/N):
Subprocessors Used:

------------------------------------------------------------
EXHIBIT B - SECURITY AND SERVICE SCHEDULE (Template)
------------------------------------------------------------
Security Contacts:
Incident Notification SLA:
Support Hours / Escalation Path:
Service Availability Target:
Backup / Restore Commitments (if applicable):
Evidence Shared (SOC report date, cert links):

------------------------------------------------------------
EXHIBIT C - VENDOR-SPECIFIC CLAUSES (Select/Add)
------------------------------------------------------------
Choose relevant clause blocks based on vendor type and risk profile.

[A] Cloud Infrastructure Vendor (e.g., AWS)
- Vendor maintains region-level controls and resilience for agreed deployment regions (for Kahana currently expected in us-east-1 unless otherwise approved).
- Vendor will provide security and compliance documentation suitable for Kahana SOC 2 evidence needs.
- Vendor shall notify Kahana of material service deprecations or security-impacting architecture changes.

[B] Database / Backend Platform Vendor (e.g., Supabase)
- Vendor shall maintain controls for database security, authentication, and storage protection.
- Vendor shall provide backup/restore capabilities and retention details applicable to contracted plan/tier.
- Vendor shall support export/deletion workflows for Kahana data at offboarding.

[C] Payment Processor (e.g., Stripe)
- Vendor shall maintain controls suitable for payment processing security and fraud monitoring.
- Vendor shall provide incident response coordination for payment-related security events affecting Kahana.

[D] Analytics Provider (e.g., Mixpanel)
- Vendor shall support configuration minimizing collection of unnecessary personal data.
- Vendor shall provide retention/deletion controls and event-level governance settings.

[E] AI Service Provider (e.g., Gemini, Deepgram)
- Vendor shall describe model data handling, retention, and training-use posture for submitted prompts/content.
- Vendor shall provide controls for API key protection, tenancy boundaries, and data confidentiality.
- Vendor shall support restrictions on prohibited data classes if configured by Kahana.

[F] Identity/SSO Provider (e.g., Okta, enterprise auth partner)
- Vendor shall enforce strong identity and access controls, including MFA and policy enforcement features.
- Vendor shall provide audit logs and administrative security controls needed for incident investigations.

------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNATURES
------------------------------------------------------------
Kahana Labs, Inc.
Name:
Title:
Date:
Signature:

Vendor:
